commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=51a4ab7eae581c78b7270648b7ec464e8623dc47)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds 11 bundled provider IDs/aliases to the model-provider overlay allowlist and adds a schema test matrix for timeout-only overlays without baseUrl/models.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +24. Total +35 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: parse a config with `models.providers.z.ai.timeoutSeconds` and no baseUrl/models, and the current schema still treats it as a custom provider. I did not run a live config-load command because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The code repair is clear, but the external PR still needs contributor-provided real config-load proof, so this should stay in normal PR review until the branch and proof are updated.

target.title: fix(config): accept overlays for bundled provider aliases
target.body:
## Summary

**What problem does this PR solve?**
Some bundled model providers and aliases from extension manifests were missing from `BUILT_IN_MODEL_PROVIDER_OVERLAY_IDS`. A config such as `models.providers.qwen-oauth.timeoutSeconds` was therefore treated as a custom provider overlay and rejected for missing `baseUrl`/`models`, even though the provider is bundled.

**What is the intended outcome?**
All bundled provider ids and manifest aliases can be configured with lightweight overlays such as `timeoutSeconds` without redefining the full provider catalog entry.

## Concrete Fix Approach

- Compare bundled extension manifest provider ids against `BUILT_IN_MODEL_PROVIDER_OVERLAY_IDS`.
- Add the missing bundled ids/aliases to the allowlist: `claude-cli`, `gmi`, `gmi-cloud`, `gmicloud`, `novita`, `novita-ai`, `novitaai`, `ollama-cloud`, `qwen-cli`, `qwen-oauth`, and `qwen-portal`.
- Keep the existing validation rule for true custom providers: they still must declare both `baseUrl` and `models`.
- Add schema coverage that each newly allowed bundled provider accepts a lightweight overlay without `baseUrl` or `models`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** bundled provider overlays were rejected as custom providers when the provider id was present in plugin manifests but absent from the schema allowlist.
- **Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw source checkout on this branch.
- **Exact command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  12 passed (12)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** representative bundled ids (`qwen-oauth`, `ollama-cloud`, `novita-ai`, `gmi`, `claude-cli`) now accept `timeoutSeconds`-only overlays.
- **What was not tested:** live provider calls; this is schema validation only.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/config/zod-schema.core.ts src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/config/zod-schema.core.ts src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts`

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes, previously rejected bundled provider overlays now validate.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** Config validation is relaxed only for manifest-declared bundled provider ids/aliases.
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** No.
- **Highest-risk area:** accidentally treating a truly custom provider as bundled; mitigated by only adding ids already declared by bundled extension manifests.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
target.author: pluviobyte
